fields.description:
# S1 — Terraform base: EC2 box, networking, DNS subdomain, IAM, SSM secrets, state/backups

## Why
The PoC needs one cost-conscious, reproducible AWS host for Gerrit + the rebar MCP.
Codifying it as IaC is runtime-free and delivers the freeze-and-restore RTO (rebuild
from Terraform) without adding cost — so it serves P3 (reusability) without touching
P2 (cost). Everything else in this epic lands on this substrate.

## What
- One-time bootstrap (`infra/bootstrap/`): an S3 state bucket (versioning + SSE) created
  by a small standalone config applied ONCE manually — this breaks the state-backend
  chicken-and-egg (TF can't store state in a bucket it hasn't created yet). Its bucket
  name feeds the main config's backend block.
- `infra/terraform/` using roll-your-own raw resources (the ec2-instance module adds
  indirection at N=1):
  - `versions.tf`: `required_version = ">= 1.10"` (S3 native `use_lockfile` needs TF
    >=1.10; older TF SILENTLY ignores it and runs with NO locking) + `required_providers`
    (aws ~> 5).
  - `t4g.large` (Graviton, ~8 GB) on AL2023 arm64 — AMI resolved via the SSM public param
    `/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-default-arm64` (NOT a hardcoded
    id); gp3 root + a separate gp3 data volume mounted at `/var/gerrit` with
    `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }`; IMDSv2 required.
  - Default VPC; security group inbound 443 (HTTPS) + 29418 (Gerrit SSH); admin access via
    SSM Session Manager (no inbound 22). One Elastic IP, also `prevent_destroy = true`.
  - Route 53 A record `rebar.solutions.navateam.com` -> EIP in the existing public zone
    `Z05558453EZPQLHKC20IQ` (solutions.navateam.com).
  - IAM instance role: attach `AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore` (REQUIRED for Session Manager
    — the sole admin path) + SSM Parameter Store read (+ KMS decrypt) + CloudWatch basic.
  - SSM Parameter Store SecureString params (placeholders) for the Gerrit secrets, the
    GitHub replication deploy key, the rebar MCP HMAC signing key, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
  - `user_data.sh` (cloud-init): at first boot, fetch the SSM SecureString params via the
    instance role and write them to a `chmod 600` `.env` (NO plaintext secrets in user_data
    itself); IMDSv2 enforced.
  - Terraform S3 backend with `use_lockfile=true` (no DynamoDB); DLM daily EBS snapshots of
    the data volume with a bounded `retain_rule { count = 7 }`.
- Packaging: ensure `infra/` is excluded from ALL distribution channels.

## Scope
In: the bootstrap + TF base + DNS subdomain + IAM + SSM scaffolding + boot script +
state/backup config + the packaging exclusion. Out: Gerrit/MCP containers (S2), Gerrit
config (S3), replication (S5), GitHub lockdown (S6).

## Files
- `infra/bootstrap/main.tf` (one-time state bucket: versioning + SSE)
- `infra/terraform/{versions,main,network,iam,ssm,dns,backup}.tf`
- `infra/terraform/user_data.sh` (SSM-pull -> chmod 600 .env boot script)
- `pyproject.toml` (`[tool.hatch.build.targets.sdist]` include is `["src/rebar","tests","README.md"]`
  so `infra/` is excluded by default — add an explicit exclude + a build-artifact check)
- `docs/adr/0012-iac-foundations.md` (ADR capturing the three novel decisions: the S3 state-bucket bootstrap chicken-and-egg pattern, the no-DynamoDB S3-native locking choice gated on Terraform >=1.10, and the `/rebar/prod/*` SSM parameter naming contract — each with rationale)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `terraform -chdir=infra/terraform validate` and `plan` succeed (TF >=1.10 enforced via
      `required_version`); `apply` stands up a reachable `t4g.large` with EIP, gp3 root + data
      volume mounted at `/var/gerrit`, IMDSv2 enforced.
- [ ] The state-bucket bootstrap (`infra/bootstrap/`) is documented as a one-time manual apply;
      the main config's S3 backend uses `use_lockfile=true` (no DynamoDB), gated by `required_version >= 1.10`.
- [ ] The stateful data volume AND the EIP carry `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` (no silent
      destroy/recreate of Gerrit data on apply/rename/AMI change).
- [ ] `rebar.solutions.navateam.com` resolves (Route 53 A -> EIP in zone Z05558453EZPQLHKC20IQ); SG
      allows 443 + 29418; admin access via SSM Session Manager with the role granting
      `AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore` and no inbound 22.
- [ ] `user_data.sh` pulls the SSM SecureString params at boot to a `chmod 600` file; no plaintext
      secrets appear in user_data.
- [ ] DLM creates daily snapshots of the data volume with bounded retention (e.g. `retain_rule count=7`).
- [ ] `docs/adr/0012-iac-foundations.md` exists and records the three novel architectural decisions with rationale: (1) the S3 state-bucket bootstrap chicken-and-egg pattern, (2) the no-DynamoDB S3-native `use_lockfile` locking choice gated on Terraform >=1.10, and (3) the `/rebar/prod/*` SSM parameter naming contract.
- [ ] A built sdist AND wheel contain NO `infra/` files (deterministic check: `python -m build` then
      grep the artifacts for `infra/`). Homebrew + the MCP registry INHERIT this exclusion because both
      package the same built artifact (the formula builds from the sdist/release; the registry ships the
      wheel) — so the sdist/wheel check is the single source of truth, documented as such.

## Round-7 remediation
- **DLM service role (G6):** AWS Data Lifecycle Manager assumes its OWN IAM role (trust principal `dlm.amazonaws.com`, perms `ec2:CreateSnapshot`/`CreateTags`/`DescribeVolumes`/`DeleteSnapshot`). S1 creates this DLM service role and wires its ARN into the `aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy` `execution_role_arn` — without it DLM silently creates no snapshots.
- [ ] The DLM lifecycle policy references a created DLM service role (trust `dlm.amazonaws.com`) and is ENABLED (verified it would snapshot the data volume, not silently no-op).

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11, 2026-06-29)
- SSM SecureString scaffolding extended so no downstream lookup fails: ADD `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` (consumed by S7 `monitoring.tf`) and `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` (consumed by S4a) to the enumerated placeholder params.
- Ownership contracts: S1 CREATES the single EC2 instance role (S2/S4a only add separately-named scoped inline policies, never re-declare it). S1 OWNS the DLM service role + `aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy` (retain count=7); S7 only asserts/monitors it (no second DLM resource).

Added ACs:
- [ ] SSM placeholder set includes `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` and `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` (in addition to Gerrit secrets, deploy key, HMAC key, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- [ ] The EC2 instance role and the DLM lifecycle policy + DLM service role are created HERE and only here; ADR-0012 records the single-owner contract (S2/S4a attach scoped policies; S7 only monitors DLM)
